hypotheses.label: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural splice/recurse mutators place MORE DISTINCT coherent structural tokens/constructs per seed than cmplog's I2S byte-substitution can, even though cmplog can place individual literals

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Use joint_necessity's GENERIC 2-arm analyze_pair (--winner-fuzzer grimoire --loser-fuzzer cmplog), NOT the default vpc/cmplog/vp 3-arm mode. The decisive pair for this shape is grimoire>cmplog (every card's covers_pairs == 'grimoire>cmplog (grimoire_structural)'), so the blocking arm is CMPLOG, not naive. analyze_pair emits canonical winner_tags/loser_tags/tag_lift/size_lift plus armtag aliases grimoire_tags/cmplog_tags. tag_lift is a DIFFERENCE (grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags) of mean distinct-token counts; size_lift is the median-size RATIO grimoire/cmplog.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + sfnt/ttc magic ('true','ttcf','OTTO') the GeneralizationStage gaps-and-preserves as coherent runs. The per-target token vocab is a measurement PARAM, not a new descriptor.

hypotheses.prediction: grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags >= 1.0: a grimoire seed carries on average at least one MORE distinct sfnt-structural token than a cmplog blocking seed. The discriminating alternative this rules out is BYTE-RICHNESS-NOT-STRUCTURE: cmplog runs I2S, so if the advantage were merely placing magic/tag LITERALS (a byte-level capability cmplog ALSO has via input-to-state), cmplog_tags would match grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0. A positive tag_lift against the I2S arm (not the naive arm) isolates grammar-aware multi-token RECOMBINATION from literal substitution. size_lift >= 1.0 corroborates that grimoire's coherent structure is at least as large as cmplog's stalled inputs.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: tag_lift (= grimoire_tags - cmplog_tags) >= 1.0 means grimoire seeds carry on average >=1 more distinct structural token than the I2S blocking seeds. grimoire_tags >= 2 is an absolute floor guarding against both arms being near-zero (non-discriminating). size_lift >= 1.0 requires grimoire's structure to be at least as large (it never shrinks to clear a structural gate). All three over named analyze_pair metrics only.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: cmplog's I2S can substitute the 4-byte table-tag literals directly, so if the gate were cleared by placing the tag LITERAL rather than by recombining a coherent multi-table structure, cmplog_tags would equal or exceed grimoire_tags and tag_lift -> 0, refuting the hypothesis. Rules out 'grimoire wins only because it places the magic literal (a byte capability cmplog shares)' and 'grimoire blocks-then-resolves for an unrelated scheduling/energy reason while both corpora carry equal structure'.
hypotheses.g2_independence: The shape's label SOURCE is the resolve pattern (grimoire resolves / cmplog blocks). The verification SIGNAL is the distinct-structural-token count + median size of the seed BYTES, never re-reading whether the branch flipped. Token presence is a byte property orthogonal to the resolve outcome.

hypotheses.label: fallback: grimoire reaches the gate via larger/deeper structure (state-machine depth, repeated category blocks, nested clusters) without a countable target-vocabulary tag signal

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: Same grimoire-vs-cmplog repoint; consumes only the size_lift metric (a subset of analyze_pair's output). For harfbuzz branches whose gate is a STATE/DEPTH property (Indic/Thai state-machine cases) rather than a named table tag.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.3 (grimoire seeds materially larger/deeper) even when tag_lift is ~0, because the gate is about state-machine DEPTH / repeated category structure rather than a countable sfnt table tag. A byte-havoc-only alternative predicts size_lift ~ 1.0 (no systematic size advantage); I2S-only (cmplog) also has no structural-growth bias, so a size advantage over the I2S arm discriminates structural recombination from local literal fixing.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: Consulted by the arbiter ONLY when structural_token_richness does NOT fire (its tag signal is flat) -- see decision_order. Catches the state-machine-depth branches the table-tag vocab misses.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: If grimoire and cmplog seeds are the same size, size_lift -> ~1.0 and this is refuted.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Median seed size is a byte-length property of the corpus, independent of the resolve label.

hypotheses.label: grimoire's grammar mutators place structural tokens/constructs into the corpus that naive's byte havoc cannot, building larger/deeper inputs

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: joint_necessity's default arms are vpc-resolving / cmplog-blocking / vp-blocking. For this shape repoint resolving_arm=grimoire, blocking_arm=naive (a param, no code change). The metrics carry over: tag_lift = grimoire_tags vs naive_tags; size_lift = median grimoire seed size vs median naive seed size; naive_tags = structural-token count in the naive (blocking) seed set. The arbiter reads grimoire_tags, naive_tags, tag_lift, size_lift.

params.tokens_note: harfbuzz tokens are the 4-byte sfnt table tags + magic the GeneralizationStage preserves. libxml2 tokens are XML/DTD structural constructs grimoire splices that naive byte havoc does not. The per-target token vocab is a measurement param; the descriptor is target-agnostic.

hypotheses.prediction: naive_tags ~ 0 (byte havoc places essentially no coherent structural tokens) AND grimoire_tags >> naive_tags (grammar mutators assemble the constructs) AND size_lift >= 1.0 (grimoire seeds are at least as large/deep, often larger). A pure-byte-mutation alternative (no grammar awareness) would predict naive_tags ~ grimoire_tags and tag_lift ~ 0, so a positive tag_lift discriminates grammar-aware assembly from byte havoc.
hypotheses.rule: tag_lift >= 1.0 AND naive_tags < grimoire_tags AND grimoire_tags >= 2
hypotheses.rule_explanation: tag_lift >= 1.0 means grimoire carries >= ~2x the structural tokens of naive; the absolute floor grimoire_tags >= 2 guards against both arms being near-zero. naive_tags < grimoire_tags enforces the directional gap.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: if naive's havoc-derived corpus happened to carry as many table tags / XML constructs as grimoire's, tag_lift -> 0 and the hypothesis is refuted. Rules OUT 'naive blocks for an unrelated reason (coverage scheduling, energy) while both corpora carry equal structure'.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape: this test measures the STRUCTURAL CONTENT of the seed corpora (token count + size), never re-reads the resolve/block outcome.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 2): grimoire's structural recombination drives the gated branch LINE deeper -- it builds the well-nested element/state-machine structure that advances the shaper/parser INTO the gated case (Indic syllable state, glyf-extents return) at high per-seed execution count, while cmplog's byte-corrupted input reaches the line far fewer times or never enters the case at all

registry_tool_repointed_arms.note: depth_reach RUNS the grimoire-resolving (W) vs cmplog-blocking (L) seeds through the libafl-harfbuzz-cov image and reads the branch line's per-seed-normalized execution count + blocked-side count from llvm-cov. --winner-fuzzer grimoire --loser-fuzzer cmplog. Both arms have DB seeds for all three branches (5440 grimoire n=10 / cmplog n=10; 5810 grimoire n=2 / cmplog n=10; 5866 grimoire n=2 / cmplog n=10) AND per_role_coverage W/L dumps already exist under db/per_role_coverage/harfbuzz/{5440,5810,5866}/{W,L}/.

hypotheses.prediction: winner_depth (grimoire per-seed execution count of the gated branch line) substantially exceeds loser_depth (cmplog), depth_lift >= 1.5 with winner_deeper=true. The state-machine cases (5810 line 531 'case 19', 5866 line 982 'case indic_symbol_cluster') are entered ONLY when the input is well-nested enough to advance the syllable machine into that state -- the per-role dumps show the grimoire (W) corpus executes the case line (29-30 hits) while the cmplog (L) corpus executes it 0 times. For 5440 (glyf-extents return-check, line 390) the W corpus drives the return-true line deeper (59 vs 31) because grimoire assembles a valid glyf+loca table the extents lookup actually traverses. side_lift corroborates that the gated true-side is reached far more by the winner corpus.
hypotheses.rule_explanation: winner_deeper requires the grimoire corpus to reach the gated branch line strictly deeper than the cmplog corpus; depth_lift >= 1.5 requires that advantage to be at least 50% (well above llvm-cov per-seed noise). Both over named depth_reach metrics only. This is a DIFFERENT measurement AXIS from the joint_necessity token/size rules above -- it measures how far each corpus DRIVES the gated line (iteration/reach depth), not how many structural tokens the seed bytes contain. It catches exactly the round-1 rule_not_met branches whose construct is encoded as state-machine DEPTH / parse-reach rather than a countable sfnt table tag (so the token rule saw grimoire_tags below floor) and whose seed sizes are not 1.3x larger (so the size fallback also missed them).
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable: Could come out the other way: if cmplog's corpus reaches the gated case line as often as grimoire's (depth_lift ~ 1, winner_deeper=false), then both corpora drive the state machine equally deep and the block must be a DOWNSTREAM value check, not a structural-reach difference -- refuting this mechanism. Rules out the alternative 'cmplog reaches the same depth but is blocked by a local value comparison the I2S arm cannot satisfy' (which would itself be an i2s/value mechanism, not grimoire-structural-reach). A positive depth_lift against the I2S arm isolates grammar-aware structural REACH from local literal fixing.
hypotheses.g2_independence: The label SOURCE is the resolve pattern (grimoire resolves / cmplog blocks at this branch). The verification SIGNAL is the per-seed-normalized EXECUTION COUNT of the branch line when each corpus is replayed through the cov binary -- a dynamic-reach property of the corpus bytes, never a re-read of the resolve/block flag. depth_reach measures what each arm's corpus REACHES, not whether the studied branch flipped.
hypotheses.novelty_note: G1-novelty vs round 1: depth_reach is the registry tool NOT yet tried on this shape (round 1 used only joint_necessity token/size counting). This is a genuinely different mechanism+measurement -- iteration/reach DEPTH of the gated line, not structural-token content of the seed -- so it is NOT a relaxed-threshold re-test of the refuted token/size rules. It is the correct axis for the three state-machine/parse-reach harfbuzz branches the round-1 token+size rules legitimately could not fire on.

data_realities_respected: depth_reach RUNS seeds through the cov binary (it does NOT use mutation_op / lineage), so the 'I2S not tagged' and 'value_profile is a feedback not a mutator' realities do not constrain it.

harfbuzz IS in the on-disk canonical-corpus set (curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3) AND has a libafl-harfbuzz-cov image AND per_role W/L dumps for 5440/5810/5866 -- all inputs depth_reach needs are present.

structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED winning vs blocking seeds, so byte-alignment (byte_freq_ratio / operand_enrichment) is NOT proposed for this shape; struct_size_ratio / token_count / depth_reach are used instead.

libxml2 is NOT on-disk and has NO grimoire-instrumented campaign anywhere (rq3 libafl is generic cmplog-only), so neither joint_necessity nor depth_reach can score the 36 libxml2 branches -- they stay decidable:false.

harfbuzz.evidence: DB carries grimoire-RESOLVING seeds for all 6 AND cmplog-BLOCKING seeds for all 6. The token/size rules (joint_necessity) confirmed 5311/5958/6181 in round 1. Round 2 ADDS depth_reach to cover the three round-1 rule_not_met branches 5440/5810/5866, whose construct is state-machine DEPTH / parse-reach (5810 'case 19' / 5866 'case indic_symbol_cluster' / 5440 glyf-extents return) not a countable sfnt table tag. per_role W/L dumps exist for all three; the dumps already show the gated case line executes in the grimoire (W) corpus and 0 times in the cmplog (L) corpus for 5810/5866, and W=59 vs L=31 for 5440.
harfbuzz.round1_delta: round 1 fired only joint_necessity (token+size). Round 2 keeps those three rules verbatim AND adds structural_iteration_depth_reach (registry tool depth_reach) for the 3 branches whose mechanism is reach-depth, not token-richness or seed-size.

libxml2.reason: Verified on this server: (1) 35/36 libxml2 grimoire branches have ZERO resolving_seeds AND ZERO blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite; the lone exception libxml2_6210 carries only value_profile-resolving / value_profile_cmplog-blocking seeds -- NOT the grimoire-resolving nor cmplog-blocking arms either joint_necessity or depth_reach needs. (2) libxml2 is NOT in the on-disk canonical-corpus set. (3) The only libxml2 corpus present is out/rq3/libxml2, the RQ3 fuzzer set with a GENERIC cmplog-only libafl build -- it has NO grimoire arm, so no grimoire-vs-cmplog contrast (token, size, OR depth) can be drawn. (4) None of these 36 grimoire branch_ids have per_role_coverage W/L dumps under the current namespace. No grimoire corpus + no DB seeds => no input for any built tool.
libxml2.g3_server_note: This decidable:false is NOT merely an off-server-corpus artifact. sB owns the libxml2 corpus, but there is no GRIMOIRE-instrumented libxml2 campaign anywhere in this layout (the canonical 4-fuzzer campaign does not include libxml2; rq3's libafl is generic/cmplog-only). Arbitrating these would require BOTH a new grimoire-vs-cmplog libxml2 campaign AND a fresh seed_bisect -- neither exists. So the 36 libxml2 branches are an HONEST data limitation (decidable:false), correctly carried to fallback:inconclusive, not forced into a label by analogy. Round 2 did NOT change this verdict: the new depth_reach tool also needs a grimoire corpus + cov image neither of which exists for libxml2.

monotonic_superset_restorations.reason: round-1 replaced it with structural_token_richness, which dropped 7 sB labels. Monotonic-superset invariant: restored verbatim; appended at END of decision_order so the round-1/2 rules keep priority for their gains/relabels. Retained UNCHANGED in round 2.

notes: ROUND-2 RE-DESIGN of grimoire_structural_WL (loop-until-dry). PRIORS KEPT VERBATIM (superset/monotonic invariant): structural_token_richness, structural_size_depth_only, structural_token_assembly -- all three carried unchanged with their exact round-1 rules, so the 11 confirmed branches (7 token_assembly + 2 size_depth_only + 2 token_richness) re-validate. NEW MECHANISM (1): structural_iteration_depth_reach, using the depth_reach registry tool (the ONE built tool NOT yet tried on this shape), targeting the 3 harfbuzz round-1 rule_not_met branches (5440, 5810, 5866). These three are NOT non-discriminable -- they were rule_not_met because their construct is encoded as state-machine DEPTH / parse-reach (Indic syllable-machine cases, glyf-extents return) rather than a countable sfnt table tag (so grimoire_tags fell below the >=2 floor) and their seeds are not 1.3x larger (so the size fallback also missed). depth_reach is the correct, genuinely-different axis: it replays the grimoire-resolving (W) vs cmplog-blocking (L) corpora through libafl-harfbuzz-cov and reads the gated branch line's per-seed execution count. The per_role dumps already show the discriminating signal: 5810 'case 19' and 5866 'case indic_symbol_cluster' execute in the W corpus (29-30 hits) and 0 times in the L corpus; 5440 glyf-extents-return line W=59 vs L=31. G1-novelty: depth_reach is iteration/reach DEPTH, not token-content or seed-size, so it is NOT a relaxed-threshold re-test of the refuted token/size rules; G1-discriminating: a depth_lift ~ 1 outcome (cmplog reaches the case as deep as grimoire) would refute and instead implicate a downstream value check (an i2s/value mechanism, not grimoire-structural). G2: the signal is dynamic line-execution count of the replayed corpus, orthogonal to the resolve flag. DECIDABLE:FALSE (honest G3 data limitation): the 36 libxml2 branches (86% of the shape) remain UNMEASURABLE on the corpus/seed axis by ANY built tool -- no grimoire-instrumented libxml2 campaign exists anywhere (verified: zero DB seeds on the grimoire/cmplog arms; libxml2 not on-disk; rq3 libafl is generic cmplog-only with no grimoire arm). depth_reach does not rescue them (it also needs a grimoire corpus + cov image neither of which exists for libxml2). These carry to fallback:inconclusive as an HONEST decidable:false, not a refutation and not an off-server-only artifact (the owning server sB has the corpus but still no grimoire campaign). MERGES unchanged from round 1: the 8 prior libxml2/harfbuzz structural clusters collapse to ONE token-richness hypothesis (no real-data measurement separates 'assembles a namespace decl' from 'assembles an ATTLIST' from 'assembles a multi-table font'; all reduce to tag_lift>0). SUMMARY: priors kept = 3 (verbatim); new mechanism = 1 (structural_iteration_depth_reach via depth_reach); decidable:false = 36 libxml2 branches. After round 2 the measurable harfbuzz set is fully covered (token-richness + size-depth + reach-depth span all 6); the libxml2 residual is an honest non-discriminable data gap requiring a future grimoire-vs-cmplog libxml2 campaign (out of scope).
